title: The Whole Duty of Kittens
content:
When Human Folk at Table eat,
         A Kitten must not mew for meat,
    Or jump to grab it from the Dish,
         (Unless it happens to be fish).
meaning:
Oliver Herford's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Whole Duty of Kittens"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
